name: easyauth-emulator
description: Emulate Azure App Service / Functions / Container Apps Easy Auth locally. Auto-starts an auth proxy during VS Code debug sessions — no Azure deployment needed.

easyauth.autoStart.description: Automatically start EasyAuth Emulator when a debug session begins.

easyauth.autoStop.description: Automatically stop EasyAuth Emulator when the linked debug session ends.

easyauth.upstreamPort.description: Manually specify the upstream port. Set to null for auto-detection.

easyauth.portScanMax.description: Maximum number of consecutive ports to scan when auto-detecting the upstream port.

easyauth.portScanBase.description: Base port for port scanning when no other hint is available.

easyauth.verbose.markdownDescription: Print all resolved configuration values (secrets masked) on startup. Passes `--verbose` to the emulator process.

easyauth.privateBrowser.command.markdownDescription:
Command to launch a private/incognito browser window. The site URL is appended as the last argument.

**Examples:**
- `msedge --inprivate`
- `chrome --incognito`
- `firefox --private-window`

Leave empty to hide the private browser button.

**Not used in remote sessions** (Remote - SSH / Tunnels): a browser cannot be launched on your local PC from the remote host, so the buttons copy the URL to the clipboard instead.

easyauth.site.url.markdownDescription: `SITE_URL`: Fallback base URL of the EasyAuth gateway, used when a request carries no Host header (without trailing slash). Usually no change needed. Set an `https://` value only if a TLS-terminating front end (reverse proxy) does not send `X-Forwarded-Proto` — the dev tunnels edge sends it, so Remote - Tunnels needs no change.

easyauth.site.port.markdownDescription: `SITE_PORT`: Listen port of the EasyAuth gateway (also the public port when accessed directly).

easyauth.tls.certFile.markdownDescription: `TLS_CERT_FILE`: Path to the TLS server certificate (PEM format). Set together with `TLS_KEY_FILE` to enable HTTPS. Required for Facebook Login.

easyauth.tls.keyFile.markdownDescription: `TLS_KEY_FILE`: Path to the TLS private key (PEM format). Set together with `TLS_CERT_FILE` to enable HTTPS. Required for Facebook Login.

easyauth.defaultIdp.markdownDescription: `DEFAULT_IDP`: Default IDP when `/.auth/login` is accessed. Must match a name in the configured IDPs. Leave empty to auto-select when only one IDP is configured, or to show the selection screen when multiple IDPs are configured.

easyauth.skipAuthRoutes.markdownDescription:
`SKIP_AUTH_ROUTES`: Routes that bypass authentication and are forwarded directly to upstream. Comma-separated list of `[METHOD=]REGEX` patterns.

**Example:** `GET=^/health$,^/public/`

easyauth.debugHeadersEndpointEnabled.markdownDescription: `DEBUG_HEADERS_ENDPOINT_ENABLED`: Enable `GET /.debug/headers` endpoint to inspect computed auth headers.

enumDescriptions: Simple Icons CDN logos (requires internet access).

easyauth.entra.displayName.markdownDescription: `IDP_ENTRA_DISPLAY_NAME`: Label shown on the IDP selection screen. Default: `entra`.

easyauth.entra.clientId.markdownDescription: **Required.** `IDP_ENTRA_CLIENT_ID`: Application (client) ID registered with Microsoft Entra ID.

easyauth.entra.scopes.markdownDescription: `IDP_ENTRA_SCOPES`: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: `openid profile email`. Add delegated permission scopes as needed (e.g. `openid profile email api://<app-id>/MyScope`).

easyauth.entra.authUserIdClaim.markdownDescription: `IDP_ENTRA_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM`: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: `preferred_username`.

easyauth.entra.extraArgs.markdownDescription: `IDP_ENTRA_EXTRA_ARGS`: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line. Example: `--allowed-group=my-group --oidc-extra-audience=myapp`.

easyauth.entra.icon.markdownDescription: `IDP_ENTRA_ICON`: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a [Simple Icons](https://simpleicons.org) slug (e.g. `microsoft`) or an image URL.

easyauth.google.displayName.markdownDescription: `IDP_GOOGLE_DISPLAY_NAME`: Label shown on the IDP selection screen. Default: `google`.

easyauth.google.scopes.markdownDescription: `IDP_GOOGLE_SCOPES`: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: `openid profile email`.

easyauth.google.authUserIdClaim.markdownDescription: `IDP_GOOGLE_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM`: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: `email`.

easyauth.google.extraArgs.markdownDescription: `IDP_GOOGLE_EXTRA_ARGS`: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line.

easyauth.google.icon.markdownDescription: `IDP_GOOGLE_ICON`: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a [Simple Icons](https://simpleicons.org) slug or an image URL.

easyauth.facebook.displayName.markdownDescription: `IDP_FACEBOOK_DISPLAY_NAME`: Label shown on the IDP selection screen. Default: `facebook`.

easyauth.facebook.scopes.markdownDescription: `IDP_FACEBOOK_SCOPES`: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: `openid profile email`.

easyauth.facebook.authUserIdClaim.markdownDescription: `IDP_FACEBOOK_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM`: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: `id`.

easyauth.facebook.extraArgs.markdownDescription: `IDP_FACEBOOK_EXTRA_ARGS`: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line.

easyauth.facebook.icon.markdownDescription: `IDP_FACEBOOK_ICON`: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a [Simple Icons](https://simpleicons.org) slug or an image URL.

easyauth.apple.displayName.markdownDescription: `IDP_APPLE_DISPLAY_NAME`: Label shown on the IDP selection screen. Default: `apple`.

easyauth.apple.scopes.markdownDescription: `IDP_APPLE_SCOPES`: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: `openid profile email`.

easyauth.apple.authUserIdClaim.markdownDescription: `IDP_APPLE_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM`: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: `email`.

easyauth.apple.extraArgs.markdownDescription: `IDP_APPLE_EXTRA_ARGS`: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line.

easyauth.apple.icon.markdownDescription: `IDP_APPLE_ICON`: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a [Simple Icons](https://simpleicons.org) slug or an image URL.

easyauth.github.displayName.markdownDescription: `IDP_GITHUB_DISPLAY_NAME`: Label shown on the IDP selection screen. Default: `github`.

easyauth.github.scopes.markdownDescription: `IDP_GITHUB_SCOPES`: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: `openid profile email`.

easyauth.github.authUserIdClaim.markdownDescription: `IDP_GITHUB_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM`: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: `login`.

easyauth.github.extraArgs.markdownDescription: `IDP_GITHUB_EXTRA_ARGS`: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line.

easyauth.github.icon.markdownDescription: `IDP_GITHUB_ICON`: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a [Simple Icons](https://simpleicons.org) slug or an image URL.

name.description: IDP identifier used in IDP_LIST (e.g. 'auth0', 'salesforce'). Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only.

displayName.description: IDP_<NAME>_DISPLAY_NAME: Label shown on the IDP selection screen.

authProvider.description: IDP_<NAME>_AUTH_PROVIDER: Value set in X-MS-CLIENT-PRINCIPAL-IDP header (e.g. 'auth0').

authUserIdClaim.description: IDP_<NAME>_AUTH_USER_ID_CLAIM: JWT claim used as user ID. Default: 'sub'.

scopes.description: IDP_<NAME>_SCOPES: Space-separated OAuth2 scopes. Default: 'openid profile email'.

codeChallengeMethod.description: IDP_<NAME>_CODE_CHALLENGE_METHOD: PKCE code challenge method. Set to 'S256' when the IdP supports it. Applies to oidc kind only.

logoutEndpoint.description: IDP_<NAME>_LOGOUT_ENDPOINT: Override the IDP logout URL.

skipClaimsFromProfileUrl.description: IDP_<NAME>_SKIP_CLAIMS_FROM_PROFILE_URL: Skip fetching additional claims from the userinfo endpoint.

extraArgs.description: IDP_<NAME>_EXTRA_ARGS: Space-separated extra flags appended verbatim to the oauth2-proxy command line (e.g. '--allowed-group=my-group --oidc-extra-audience=myapp').

icon.description: IDP_<NAME>_ICON: Icon shown on the IdP selection page. Specify a Simple Icons slug (e.g. 'auth0') or an image URL.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.portBase.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_PORT_BASE`: Base port for internal oauth2-proxy instances. Each IDP uses a consecutive port starting from this value (e.g. 4180, 4181, …).

easyauth.oauth2proxy.standardLogging.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_STANDARD_LOGGING`: Show oauth2-proxy startup/shutdown messages in the Output channel.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.authLogging.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_AUTH_LOGGING`: Show oauth2-proxy authentication events in the Output channel.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.requestLogging.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_REQUEST_LOGGING`: Show oauth2-proxy per-request HTTP logs in the Output channel.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.showDebugOnError.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_SHOW_DEBUG_ON_ERROR`: Show detailed error info on OIDC failures (e.g. misconfigured client ID or issuer URL). Useful during setup.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.version.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_VERSION`: Pin oauth2-proxy to a specific version tag (e.g. `v7.6.0`). Leave empty to use the latest stable version.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.autoUpdate.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_AUTO_UPDATE`: Automatically update oauth2-proxy to the latest version on startup. When false, a notification is shown if a newer version is available.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.sslCaBundle.markdownDescription: `SSL_CA_BUNDLE`: Path to a custom CA certificate bundle (PEM format). Normally not needed — the OS certificate store (Windows, macOS, Linux) is used automatically. Set this only when the required CA is not in the OS trust store, for example on Linux systems where the corporate CA cannot be added to the system store.

easyauth.oauth2proxy.trustedProxyIp.markdownDescription: `OAUTH2_PROXY_TRUSTED_PROXY_IP`: Comma-separated list of trusted reverse-proxy IP addresses or CIDRs from which `X-Forwarded-*` headers are accepted. Auto-set to `127.0.0.1,::1` when `APP_UPSTREAM` points to localhost. Set explicitly for non-local setups such as Docker (e.g. `172.17.0.0/16`).

view/title.when: view == easyauth.statusView && (easyauth.state == running || easyauth.state == starting || easyauth.state == error)

view/item/context.when: view == easyauth.statusView && (viewItem == easyauth.running || viewItem == easyauth.starting || viewItem == easyauth.error)

commands.title: Copy IDP Login URL for Private Browser
Open IDP Login in Private Browser

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run compile:tests
